exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in the box
exercises.title: A: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in the box
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in the box.

items.sentence: It was difficult to ...... Mia that the plan could work.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is persuade someone, so the correct answer is "convince".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: The manager has ...... to give us extra time for the project.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is say no, so the correct answer is "refused".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Please stop ...... about the weather and enjoy the trip!

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Stop complaining about the weather.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "doubt" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "I ...... he'll remember to bring the tickets.".

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
We should throw away these broken chairs. **rid**
We should ...... these broken chairs.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is throw away, so the correct answer is "get rid of".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "get rid of", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "get rid of" is a phrasal verb or fixed chunk that collocates with the rest of the sentence.

items.sentence:
I don't think students should use phones during lessons. **approve**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Don't approve of students using phones.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: They tried to ...... the issue before the meeting started.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is fix a problem, so the correct answer is "solve".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Marta came ...... with a clever answer to the problem.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is think of an idea, so the correct answer is "up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

items.sentence: We need to ...... with this complaint immediately.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is handle, so the correct answer is "deal".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.
